introSection: Panic Drive (Japanese: パニックドライブ) is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Nasu Highland Park in Nasu, Tochigi, Japan. Opened since 28 February 2004, it is located in the space previously occupied by Speed Bobsleigh. It was previously located at Takarazuka Family Land where it was known as Skip Coaster Kowa-Kowa Car?! (Japanese: スキップコースター こわこわカー！？). It eventually closed with the park on 7 April 2003.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Blue track and pink supports. The ride used to feature mint track.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are designed to resemble classic Ford cars.

sections.title: External links
sections.content:
* Panic Drive on the parks website (Japanese)
 * Official Sansei PDF featuring the coaster

fullText:
Panic Drive (Japanese: パニックドライブ) is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Nasu Highland Park in Nasu, Tochigi, Japan. Opened since 28 February 2004, it is located in the space previously occupied by Speed Bobsleigh. It was previously located at Takarazuka Family Land where it was known as Skip Coaster Kowa-Kowa Car?! (Japanese: スキップコースター こわこわカー！？). It eventually closed with the park on 7 April 2003.



Blue track and pink supports. The ride used to feature mint track.

The trains are designed to resemble classic Ford cars.




 * Panic Drive on the parks website (Japanese)
 * Official Sansei PDF featuring the coaster
